title: Singapore Market Proof
summary: Evidence set for explaining why Joz is strong for Singapore-market roles across AI, UX systems, enterprise delivery, and spatial computing.
body:
Singapore Market Proof

Purpose
This record gives Joz LLM a compact evidence set for skill and recruiter questions about why Joz is relevant for Singapore-market roles.

Proof set
- Maybank-Ageas Etiqa: Joz helped drive 20x digital sales growth through conversational and ML-led UX.
- Manulife: Joz established a Lean ML UX practice across 11 APAC markets and launched first-in-market ML UX solutions from Singapore.
- Mediacorp: Joz contributed to 30x audience growth and built a global experience language across 30+ products.
- Singapore Stock Exchange: Joz re-engineered portal experiences in a regulated-market context.
- Apple/Pixar-adjacent work in Singapore: Joz developed Python USD(z) and computer-vision workflows tied to spatial computing and 3D pipelines.

Skill interpretation
- These examples support AI architecture, ML product delivery, UX systems thinking, design-system execution, regulated-enterprise delivery, and spatial-computing credibility.
- The value is not just "worked in Singapore." The value is measurable outcome plus technical depth inside Singapore-relevant environments.

Answering rule
- Use this proof set when asked why Joz is strong for Singapore roles or Singapore recruiters.
- Do not inject Singapore into generic skills answers unless the prompt asks for it.

verification.notes: Compiled from verified user-approved positioning and programme evidence already used in Joz LLM answers.

claims: Joz has measurable Singapore-market proof across banking, insurance, media, exchange platforms, and spatial computing.
The strongest Singapore proof spans Maybank-Ageas Etiqa, Manulife, Mediacorp, Singapore Exchange, and Apple/Pixar-adjacent work.
This proof supports both recruiter positioning and skill explanation for Singapore roles.
proof_points: Manulife: Lean ML UX practice across 11 APAC markets from Singapore.
Mediacorp: 30x audience growth and a global experience language across 30+ products.
Apple/Pixar-adjacent work: Python USD(z), computer vision, and spatial-computing workflows in Singapore.
